_comment: Ground-truth pairs for the code prompt->profile classifier, ANONYMIZED.
Each `prompt` is a genericized SHAPE of one of the operator's real first-
names, IPs, secrets, and personal details are stripped; the task's weight is
preserved. `op_model`/`op_thinking` are the operator's ACTUAL settings when
they sent that prompt (the ground truth we score against). op_model is a short
omp model id (see omp/models.yml) or "default"; op_thinking is the level or
"default" (omp default = gpt-5.6-sol:medium). Regenerate the raw source with
extract_groundtruth.py; this file is curated by hand so no verbatim operator
content ships. Pure terminal noise (accidental pastes, bare paths, stray
keystrokes) was dropped; a few near-duplicate shapes were collapsed.

items.prompt: Finish an infrastructure edge migration: remove an internal reverse-proxy adapter and route the service directly through the main ingress proxy. Context: storage was just moved to a new volume, and the compose stack needs to be updated to match.

Audit this site in read-only mode. Do not edit files. Focus on security, deployment assumptions, route protection, CI/test gaps, and obvious UX or maintainability risks. Start by reading the README, the manifest, and the compose files.
Do a read-only security audit of this repo. First read the open issues (the results of a previous audit) and take them into account as you go. Assume the architecture may be weak.
Could you look at my repo and give it a critical, senior-dev review — call out some of the bad decisions that were taken?
items.note: operator chose the scarce fable elite lead

items.prompt: In a new branch, revisit the whole website's style and UX into something with a better UI and better taste. Use a browser-automation tool to get visual feedback on how it looks.
Drop the current branch completely — it drifted too much — and check out back to main. Make sure the local and remote tree is clean when you're done.
items.note: light git chore run at xhigh — a likely over-provision

items.prompt: I'd like a full UI/UX redesign pass on my app using the appropriate tools (browser automation) to scan the app and all its paths/states and identify vectors of improvement, acting like a designer.
items.note: operator chose the scarce fable elite lead

items.prompt: ls
items.note: bare shell command run at smart/medium — a clear over-provision

items.prompt: Let's continue the work we left off in a previous session — check what the agent last sent me, pick up where they started, and finish it off if anything remains.
Let's pick up what we were working on in a previous session. I had another agent do the non-API tasks visually; verify it on your end to see if it's correct, then continue.
Could you continue the work that's been started on this branch and its attached issue?

Autonomously investigate and implement a high-quality fix for a linked upstream issue in a CLI tool (a reproduction and root-cause analysis are linked). You may fork the repo, branch, implement, commit, and push to my fork — but do NOT open the upstream PR or contact maintainers; yield back to me before that.
Run a quick audit of this codebase and tell me if you find anything worth mentioning or not.
items.note: same 'audit a repo' shape as others but run at the cheapest/lowest settings

items.prompt: Could you add 'docker compose' back to my dotfiles? My agents keep trying to run it and failing — it was supposedly here already but isn't anymore. Did we remove it at some point? Add it back.
Could you update my dotfiles to the latest version of one of my tools? Look at how previous updates were done in the repo and do it the same way; you can merge the PR and confirm when I can apply.
I was churning through all the issues attached to this repository with another agent, but I ran out of usage, so I'm asking you to take over and continue. Here's its final prompt before it stopped.
items.note: operator chose fable at max thinking

items.prompt: My managed settings are locked for this session, and I want to change one specific rule. Help me do it the right way.
Help me get this project up to date — I've let it fall behind. I'd like to commit, push, and merge everything current up to main so I can move on to a specific next thing with it.
state of the repo?
items.note: status query run at smart/xhigh — a clear over-provision

items.prompt: There's an error about MCP and a plugin when I start my agent tool on my computer. Could you help me troubleshoot it?
I'm running a CLI tool that hosts a website on a localhost port on a remote VPS I reach over SSH. What do I run on my local machine to access that website — how do I port-forward?
I'd like to modify a tool-use rule in my agent setup that just triggered an error. Walk me through changing it.
Using the gh CLI, could you look into my GitHub account and organization and make a general audit focused on a few of my named projects?
Could you go online and look at a tool's repository? They recently released a new command I'm not sure I understand — analyze it and explain what it does.
My agent hit a GitHub API rate-limit error while resolving a pr:// reference. Help me understand what's happening and how to fix it.
Could you quickly check my GitHub GraphQL API usage and how close or how trending I am toward hitting the rate limit?
What is this open-source project someone mentioned to me?
Could you analyze a given third-party repository and audit what technology it uses, how it does its rendering, and the logic it uses to scan a map and build smart 3D modelling around it?
Could you tell me whether I have access to all the models and providers listed in my agent tool's model menu — maybe through a CLI or API if there's one?
items.note: capability check run at smart/xhigh — a likely over-provision

items.prompt: I'd like to free up space on my machine's root disk. Could you dig in, find the space hogs, and list them clearly and concisely so I can reflect on what to get rid of?
I'm curious: can the CLI on this machine start a prompt on my laptop, which I've SSHed in from? The desktop app is running on the laptop right now.
Check a previous discussion and make sure the agent was correct in its research, conclusion, and suggestions — specifically, whether we can pilot one desktop app from another.
Could you review this PR and tell me whether everything is sound, or whether it overextended, can be simplified, forgot something, etc.?
items.note: PR review run at max thinking

items.prompt: Audit this Next.js site in read-only mode. Do not edit files. Focus on security, deployment assumptions, route protection, CI/test gaps, and obvious UX or maintainability risks. Start by reading the README, the manifest, and the config.

I want to modify/improve the access-granting dashboard of my website to work more like the beta-invite system in another repo of mine. The idea: I want my identity provider to offer login with two methods.

I want to add a little fun feature to the landing page: a left mouse click should be able to 'grab' the spinning 3D object and make it spin on its center — like grabbing a disc and throwing it with mouse movement.

Could you analyze this repository and tell me how it looks — axes of improvement, performance improvements, even the landing-page animation if there's anything. Anything goes; let's make a small audit!
Pick one of the open issues in this repository that needs operator review so we can address it together, then you can start working on it afterwards. What needs my review?
Could you audit this codebase for improvement across all dimensions — design, security, features, docs, visuals — I'm open to everything, on all kinds of files.
It's a heatwave outside and I have an air conditioner. It's 26C outside, my AC targets 16C, and my flat is about 50 square metres. Am I better off leaving a window open or keeping everything shut?
items.note: not a coding task — an everyday question typed into the coding agent; edge case

items.prompt: Set an environment variable on my machine to a value I'll give you.
items.note: original prompt embedded a live API key, stripped here
